svg.title: Exercise Categorization Model for Sports Performance
svg.desc: Two-axis model: training quality (force-velocity position) × primary joint/region. Plus intensity tier for plyometrics.
svg.text: Exercise Categorization: Two Axes That Matter
For sports performance, exercise selection answers two questions: what adaptation? and where in the body?
Axis 1 — TRAINING QUALITY (what adaptation does it drive?)
Maps directly to the force-velocity curve. This determines which block/phase an exercise gets emphasized in.
Axis 2 — PRIMARY JOINT / REGION (where in the body?)
From VJB's joint-by-joint model. Each joint has its own strength + RFD + plyo exercises. This ensures balanced programming.
Training quality tells you WHEN to use it (which phase/block). Primary joint tells you HOW MUCH to use it (balance across joints).
An AI building a program asks: "I need a hip-dominant strength-speed exercise for Block 2" → answer: Hang Power Clean. That's a 2-axis lookup.
Why enums, not a tags table?
1. These categories are stable — they map to
2. An AI agent needs fast filtering:
3. Enums enforce valid values — no typo drift
4. Tags add complexity without adding insight
"Block 1 strength day, need a knee exercise"
